seo.title: Building Research Agents with LangChain + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with LangChain + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: LangChain is the leading framework for building sophisticated LLM-powered applications like Research Agents that perform multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis. These agents excel at gathering and summarizing information but are prone to hallucinations, tool loops, context overflow, and formatting issues that can produce unreliable outputs. Vex provides essential guardrails through verification of sources, claims, recency, and bias to ensure trustworthy research results.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with LangChain
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents typically follow a ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) pattern with a central LLM executor that orchestrates tools for web search, page scraping, and content summarization. The agent receives a research query, decomposes it into sub-questions, executes parallel or sequential tool calls (e.g., Tavily search, Wikipedia lookup), retrieves relevant documents, and synthesizes findings into a coherent report. LangChain's AgentExecutor manages the loop between reasoning and tool execution, while RAG chains handle retrieval-augmented generation for claim verification.

Key components include: a research-focused LLM (e.g., GPT-4o or Claude 3.5), custom tools for search and analysis, a memory buffer for conversation history, and output parsers for structured responses. The architecture scales to handle complex queries by chunking long documents and maintaining citation tracking through LangChain's Document retrievers.

risks.name: Hallucination in RAG retrieval chains
risks.description: Agent fabricates details when search results don't perfectly match query or when combining multiple sources
risks.mitigation: Wrap retriever chains with vex.verify(source_verification=True, claim_accuracy=True)

risks.name: Tool call loops in agent executors
risks.description: Agent gets stuck repeatedly calling the same search tool without progressing to synthesis
risks.mitigation: Set max_iterations=5 and use vex.verify(loop_detection=True) on agent executor

risks.name: Context window overflow in long conversations
risks.description: Accumulated search results exceed token limits, causing truncation or degraded performance
risks.mitigation: Implement summarization buffers and verify with vex.verify(context_overflow=True)

risks.name: Inconsistent output formatting across chain steps
risks.description: Mixed structured/unstructured responses break downstream parsing and citation tracking

implementation.title: Basic Research Agent with Tavily Search
implementation.description: Core ReAct agent setup with web search tool for research queries
implementation.code:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_community.tools.tavily_search import TavilySearchResults
from langchain.agents import create_tool_calling_agent, AgentExecutor
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o", temperature=0)
tools = [TavilySearchResults(max_results=3)]

prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a world-class researcher. Use search tools to find recent, accurate information."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
    ("placeholder", "{agent_scratchpad}"),
])

agent = create_tool_calling_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
agent_executor = AgentExecutor(agent=agent, tools=tools, verbose=True, max_iterations=5)

result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": "Latest developments in quantum computing as of 2024"})
print(result['output'])

implementation.title: RAG Chain for Source Verification
implementation.description: Retrieval chain that verifies claims against retrieved documents
implementation.code:
from langchain_community.vectorstores import FAISS
from langchain_openai import OpenAIEmbeddings
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA

documents = [...]  # Search results from agent tools
text_splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(chunk_size=1000, chunk_overlap=200)
splits = text_splitter.split_documents(documents)
vectorstore = FAISS.from_documents(splits, OpenAIEmbeddings())

qa_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(
    llm=ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o"),
    chain_type="stuff",
    retriever=vectorstore.as_retriever(search_kwargs={'k': 4})
)

verified_answer = qa_chain.invoke({
    "query": "What are the key findings from these sources?",
    "context": "Verify all claims against provided documents"
})

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Agent Executor with Vex Verification
vexIntegration.description: Protects against tool loops and hallucinations with comprehensive checks
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.verify(
    source_verification=True,
    claim_accuracy=True,
    loop_detection=True,
    max_iterations=5
)
def verified_research_agent(query: str):
    result = agent_executor.invoke({"input": query})
    return result['output']

# Usage
safe_result = verified_research_agent(
    "Latest quantum computing breakthroughs 2024"
)
print(safe_result)

vexIntegration.title: RAG Chain Source + Recency Verification
vexIntegration.description: Ensures retrieved documents are recent and claims match sources

cta.heading: Secure Your LangChain Research Agents
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and wrap your agents to eliminate hallucinations, loops, and bias in production research applications.
